samples.xml_description:
<module id="parallel2serial">
    <description>Converts parallel data to serial format.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="d" width_description="[3:0]">4-bit parallel data input.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Valid signal for serial output.</output>
        <output id="dout">Serial data output.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="N">4</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="cnt" width_description="[2:0]">Counter for serial output.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="valid" width_description="[2:0]">Valid signal for serial output.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="data" width_description="[3:0]">Data register for serial output.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
    The module parallel2serial converts parallel data to serial format. 
    The module has 4-bit parallel data input (d) and a valid signal (valid_out) to indicate the availability of serial output. 
    The module has a serial data output (dout) and a clock signal (clk) and a reset signal (rst_n) for synchronous operations.
    When the counter (cnt) is 3, the module updates the data register (data) with the parallel input (d), resets the counter (cnt) to 0, and sets the valid signal (valid) to 1.
    Otherwise, the module increments the counter (cnt) by 1, sets the valid signal (valid) to 0, and shifts the data register (data) one bit to the left, with the most significant bit shifted to the least significant bit.
    </implementation>
</module>
